skill.name: when-creating-skill-template-use-skill-builder
skill.description: Generate Claude Code skill templates with YAML frontmatter, phased documentation, validation steps, and companion workflow files.
skill.summary: Build complete Claude Code skill templates with structure, documentation, and validation guidance.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged many command, crypto, sensitive-file, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found no malicious intent or prompt injection; most high-risk hits are documentation placeholders, file names, or template syntax. The remaining real concern is that the skill recommends external CLI execution through npx and local validation commands, so publication should include a warning about reviewing generated commands and dependencies.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution in Skill Templates
medium_findings.description: The skill contains multiple examples that run npx claude-flow@alpha commands and validation tools. These examples are consistent with a skill-builder workflow, but users should review third-party CLI commands before running generated templates.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external command patterns are real, and they are part of documented user workflows. Confidence is high that commands exist, but lower on exploitability because they are examples rather than hidden execution paths.

medium_findings.title: Generated Shell Script Placeholder Requires Review
medium_findings.description: The template includes a shell script placeholder that can be populated by future skill content. This is not malicious by itself, but generated shell content should be reviewed before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell template is explicit and could lead to executable output. It remains a moderate concern because no concrete malicious command is present in this skill.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Documentation
low_findings.description: The weak-crypto, sensitive-file, and reconnaissance alerts appear to come from template words, file names, GraphViz labels, phase keys, and fenced examples. No evidence found of cryptographic operations, certificate harvesting, system reconnaissance, or hidden execution logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows these locations are descriptive text, placeholders, or validation examples. There is no code path that performs crypto, secret collection, or reconnaissance.

dangerous_patterns.title: npx-Based Third-Party Tool Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill recommends npx claude-flow@alpha commands. This pattern can execute third-party package code and should be treated as a command-review requirement.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The npx commands are clearly present and executable if copied by a user. The risk is contextual because they are visible examples for an expected CLI workflow.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent skill files that follow Claude Code conventions. This skill provides a repeatable template process for structure, documentation, validation, and workflow diagrams.
actual_capabilities: Defines the required structure for a new Claude Code skill.
Shows YAML frontmatter fields for metadata, triggers, outputs, and validation.
Provides templates for SKILL.md, README.md, PROCESS.md, and process-diagram.gv.
Describes a five-phase workflow from requirements through documentation.
Includes validation examples for YAML, GraphViz, file completeness, and dry runs.
Suggests memory patterns and completion reporting for generated skills.
limitations: It is documentation and template guidance, not a standalone generator script.
It relies on claude-flow examples that users must install and trust separately.
Generated shell commands and scripts require review before execution.
It focuses on Claude Code skill structure, not general plugin development.

use_cases.title: Standardize Internal Skill Creation
use_cases.description: Create consistent skill folders with the same documentation and validation structure across a team.

use_cases.title: Teach New Contributors
use_cases.description: Give contributors a guided example of frontmatter, phases, outputs, and success criteria for new skills.

use_cases.title: Document AI Workflows
use_cases.description: Convert a repeatable AI workflow into a structured skill with a process file and workflow diagram.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to outline a new Claude Code skill for [task]. Include the name, description, category, tags, outputs, and validation criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Build the Four Core Files
prompt_templates.prompt: Create the content plan for SKILL.md, README.md, PROCESS.md, and process-diagram.gv for a skill that handles [workflow].

prompt_templates.title: Add Validation and Examples
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this draft skill and add validation steps, usage examples, success metrics, and troubleshooting notes while preserving the existing purpose.

prompt_templates.title: Convert Workflow to Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this multi-step workflow into a Claude Code skill with phases, agents, memory schema, outputs, and a process diagram. Flag any command that needs manual review.

output_examples.input: A request to create a reusable skill for generating release notes.
output_examples.output: A complete skill outline with YAML metadata, phased workflow, core files, validation checks, and expected outputs.

output_examples.input: An existing workflow that lacks documentation and success criteria.
output_examples.output: A structured skill plan that adds README guidance, PROCESS steps, validation criteria, and a workflow diagram.

output_examples.input: A team standard that requires every skill to include trigger conditions and completion reports.
output_examples.output: A template checklist that includes triggers, outputs, memory schema, completion reporting, and review points.

best_practices: Review every generated command before running it in a shell.
Keep skill phases short, named, and tied to clear validation criteria.
Use concrete outputs so users can verify when the skill is complete.
anti_patterns: Do not publish generated skills with placeholder metadata still present.
Do not run npx or shell snippets without reviewing the dependency and command arguments.
Do not omit validation steps for generated files or workflow diagrams.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It guides creation of Claude Code skill files, including SKILL.md, README.md, PROCESS.md, and a GraphViz process diagram.

faq.question: Is this a code generator?
faq.answer: It is mainly a template and workflow guide. It shows example commands and structures that an assistant can use.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: The skill includes external CLI examples using npx and shell validation commands. Users should review those commands before execution.

faq.question: Does it contain prompt injection?
faq.answer: No evidence found of prompt injection language, override instructions, or requests to bypass security review.

faq.question: Can it be used with Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The template content can guide Codex, Claude, and Claude Code when creating structured skill documentation.

faq.question: What should users check before publishing a generated skill?
faq.answer: Users should remove placeholders, validate YAML and diagrams, review shell commands, and confirm the documented workflow is accurate.
